title: Pleroma
description: Lightweight, federated (ActivityPub) social networking server - a self-hosted, resource-friendly microblogging alternative for the Fediverse.
installation: This template runs the PRIMARY Pleroma server container (the BEAM/Elixir OTP release on git.pleroma.social/pleroma/pleroma:stable, HTTP on port 4000). Pleroma is single-process: the web endpoint AND the Oban background job queues (federation delivery, scheduled tasks, media processing) all run inside this one container, so NO separate worker/scheduler companion is needed. Pleroma uses PostgreSQL ONLY - it does NOT use Redis/Valkey. REQUIRED before first boot: 1) Rotate SECRET_KEY_BASE - generate with 'openssl rand -base64 64' (or 'mix phx.gen.secret') and set it; the placeholder MUST be replaced. 2) The Postgres add-on database needs the citext, pg_trgm and uuid-ossp extensions - exec into the pleroma pod and run the DB migrations, then enable them: connect to the db and run 'CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS citext; CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm; CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";' (the official entrypoint runs './bin/pleroma_ctl migrate' on start). 3) Create the first admin AFTER migrations: exec in and run './bin/pleroma_ctl user new <nickname> <email> --admin'. CONFIG: this image is driven by an Elixir config file at PLEROMA_CONFIG_PATH (/etc/pleroma/config.exs); the official docker entrypoint generates it from the DOMAIN / INSTANCE_NAME / ADMIN_EMAIL / NOTIFY_EMAIL / DB_* / SECRET_KEY_BASE env vars set here on first run - mount the /etc/pleroma volume so the generated config persists. DOMAIN is baked into ActivityPub actor URLs and cannot be changed cleanly after federating, so set it to your real host before going live. COMPANION SERVICES: none are mandatory in-cluster (Kubero ingress provides the TLS reverse proxy that a bare-metal install would need from nginx, and handles the optional MediaProxy caching). For a production frontend you may later swap the bundled Pleroma-FE for Soapbox/Mangane by uploading it to the /var/lib/pleroma/static volume. For object storage at scale, point Pleroma's Uploaders.S3 config (AWS_* / S3 settings in config.exs) at an external S3 bucket (e.g. the RustFS add-on) instead of the local uploads volume; omit it here for a single-node start.
